title: Phase 2: SessionStart hook adds stale-claim + dirty-tree summaries
description:
Phase 2 of docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. Blocked qualitatively on the Phase 1 → Phase 2 gate.

Extend .claude/hooks/session-start.sh (which currently ships only the staleness ping) with:
- bd list --claimed-by-me filtered to claims older than 1 hour → one-line summary.
- git status --short if non-empty → one-line summary.

Hook stays emit-only. The main-session contract on what to do with the output (resume / abandon / inspect prompt) is already documented in docs/agent-workflow.md → "Main-session contract on SessionStart hook output" section. No skill changes needed; just the hook body grows.

The hook body in main as of #74 already structures the cases for extension.
notes:
plan: docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md Phase 2 (gate: qualitative, ~10 real /bd-execute runs)
verify: bash .claude/hooks/session-start.sh < <(echo '{}') && echo ok — and a manual dirty-tree test the user runs after claiming a sacrificial bd issue

title: bd-close-reviewed: handle already-closed bd issue gracefully
description:
scripts/bd-close-reviewed.sh --phase=close calls `bd close <id>` without checking whether the issue is already closed. If a controller invokes the skill twice (interrupted session, manual re-run, network blip), `bd close` may error or no-op depending on bd version.

Make it explicitly idempotent: check `bd show <id> --json` for status=closed first; exit 0 silently if already closed (with a one-line stdout note). The override-recording path (--override-block) should also short-circuit on already-closed (don't append another override line, don't create another empty trailer commit).

Verify is weak as filed (needs a sacrificial closed bd issue to exercise the dual-close path); tighten when implementing.
notes:
plan: trivial — small script change; defensive only
verify: bash scripts/test/bd-close-reviewed-idempotent.test.sh

title: Phase 2: bd-close-reviewed adds --phase=warnings step (warnings:check)
description:
Phase 2 of docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. Blocked-by warnings baseline (task story-editor-cgg).

Once the warnings baseline lands:
- scripts/bd-close-reviewed.sh grows a --phase=warnings step that runs `npm run warnings:check`.
- .claude/skills/bd-close-reviewed/SKILL.md gains a step 1.5 to invoke it before close.
- Refuse close on new-warning regressions (same shape as typecheck refusal in step 1).

A Phase-2-deferred placeholder note already exists in bd-close-reviewed/SKILL.md.
notes:
plan: docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md Phase 2
verify: bash scripts/bd-close-reviewed.sh dummy --phase=warnings

title: Phase 2: warnings baseline + npm scripts (baseline / baseline:add / check)
description:
Phase 2 of docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. Blocked qualitatively on the Phase 1 → Phase 2 gate (~10 real /bd-execute runs without main-session rule-restating; per plan Phase 1 verification step 7).

Add:
- .warnings-baseline.json (single file; site hash per warning, not line-bound).
- npm run warnings:baseline — generates the baseline.
- npm run warnings:baseline:add — adds a single new warning to the baseline (explicit acknowledgement gate).
- npm run warnings:check — exit 0 against current baseline; non-zero with the new site reported when a fresh warning appears.

Goal: prevent new warnings from hiding behind legacy ones during the existing-warning sprawl. See plan Phase 2 Verification step 9.
notes:
plan: docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md Phase 2 (gate: qualitative, ~10 real /bd-execute runs)
verify: npm run warnings:baseline && npm run warnings:check

title: lint:design — fail on bare rgb()/hsl()/oklch()/hwb() calls in frontend/src/**
description:
Phase 1 leftover from docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. The plan calls for lint:design to fail on raw hex / rgb() / hsl() colour values so themeable colours go through the --ink-*/--bg-* tokens in frontend/src/index.css.

Current state of frontend/scripts/lint-design.mjs (verified 2026-05-06):
- Pattern 4 already catches Tailwind arbitrary-value escapes: bg-[#fff], text-[rgb(...)], text-[hsl(...)], oklch, hwb.
- Pattern 5 already catches raw hex codes anywhere in source: #abc through #abcdef12.

What's still missing: bare rgb()/hsl()/oklch()/hwb() function calls outside Tailwind brackets — e.g. inside style={{color: 'rgb(...)'}} or string constants like const c = 'hsl(...)'. Add patterns for those.

Out-of-scope: AST-aware style={{...}} detection scoped to colour/spacing/typography (separate engineering task per the plan's CLAUDE.md migration scope; until then, code-quality-reviewer + frontend rules digest catches inline-style misuse at review time).
notes:
plan: trivial — single-file regex addition; no design discussion needed
verify: cd frontend && node scripts/lint-design.mjs

title: Migrate verify: lines from TASKS.md into bd, retire /task-verify dependency on TASKS.md
description: Migrate working task tracking to bd entirely. End state: TASKS.md is historical only; verify-as-contract preserved (verify lines in bd --notes); /task-verify rewritten to read from bd; bd close gated by a new bd-close-verified wrapper; pre-tasks-edit hook + four TASKS.md helper scripts retired. See plan for phased approach (build bd-aware tooling in parallel; cut over atomically; soak + close).
notes:
plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-06-bd-tooling-migration.md
verify: [ ! -f .claude/hooks/pre-tasks-edit.sh ] && [ ! -f scripts/extract-verify.sh ] && [ ! -f scripts/tasks-proposed.sh ] && [ ! -f scripts/tasks-implementable.sh ] && [ -x scripts/bd-close-verified.sh ] && ! grep -q 'pre-tasks-edit' .claude/settings.json && grep -q 'bd-close-verified' CLAUDE.md
ref: this is the meta-issue tracking the migration of /task-verify, pre-tasks-edit.sh, extract-verify.sh, tasks-proposed.sh, tasks-implementable.sh, and TASKS.md itself onto bd.

title: [M3] Track @hono/node-server advisory GHSA-92pp-h63x-v22m
description: Path-traversal in serveStatic via repeated slashes. Pulled in transitively as prisma → @prisma/dev → @hono/node-server@<1.19.13. @prisma/dev powers prisma studio / prisma dev — dev-only surface, not loaded by @prisma/client at runtime. Production hosts (only run prisma migrate deploy) not exposed. Periodically re-run cd backend && npm audit and bump prisma / @prisma/client / @prisma/adapter-pg together when clean version available. Do NOT take 'npm audit fix --force' — proposes prisma@6.19.3, a major-version downgrade. Close when audit advisory disappears without downgrade, or when underlying advisory is withdrawn.
notes:
verify: cd backend && bash -c 'npm audit --omit=dev --json 2>/dev/null | jq ".vulnerabilities | to_entries | map(select(.value.via[] | type==\"object\" and .source==1107173)) | length"' | tee /tmp/x21-count && [ "$(cat /tmp/x21-count)" = "0" ]
ref: TASKS.md [M3] (was X21) (backlog next)

title: [M2] Finish act() warning sweep in frontend tests
description: X15 was ticked but verbose run still emits 60 'An update to <Component> inside a test was not wrapped in act(...)' lines across 9 files: AppShell, ChatComposer, ChatPanel, InlineAIResult, ModelPicker, SelectionBubble, Settings.appearance, Settings.models, Sidebar. Two root causes: (a) TanStack Query settle-after-test — useUserSettingsQuery resolves and fires setSizeDraft/setTweaks etc. inside seed useEffects after test last await returns; fix per-test by waitFor or settle-helper in tests/setup.ts; (b) Zustand setState from outside React combined with useLayoutEffect-driven re-positioning (SelectionBubble.tsx:157). X15 fix pattern (await userEvent.click / await screen.findBy / await waitFor) is correct — apply consistently.
notes:
verify: cd frontend && bash -c 'npm run test:frontend -- --run --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -c "not wrapped in act"' | tee /tmp/act-count && [ "$(cat /tmp/act-count)" = "0" ]
ref: TASKS.md [M2] (was X20) (backlog next)
partial: PR landed `router.tsx` lazy-ReactQueryDevtools gate for test mode — kills 14 of 60 "suspended resource finished loading" warnings. 52 remain across 9 components.
remaining-scope: Each remaining cluster needs production-code investigation, not just test-pattern application.
  - SelectionBubble (12): useLayoutEffect calls setPos() after a zustand useSyncExternalStore notification; the standard X15 patterns (await findBy / await waitFor / await act(async)) don't catch the follow-up setPos. Likely needs component refactor (drop separate `pos` state; compute inline OR ref-driven imperative DOM write) so post-mount setState goes away.
  - ThemeApply (5), InlineAIResult (9), DisplayNameSection (10): TanStack Query + useEffect post-commit pattern. The query observer subscription settles after the test's act batch and the effect's setState lands outside. Needs the same kind of architectural fix or a settle helper that's TanStack-Query-aware.
  - ChatMessages (4), CastTab (4), AppShell (4), Sidebar (2), ChatComposer (2): not investigated yet, likely similar.
  Verified by hand 2026-05-07 that switching tests to `await act(async)` / `await screen.findBy*` / `createQueryClient()` (with default staleTime: 30s) does NOT change the count — the standard X15 patterns are exhausted.

title: [M1] Resolve pg@8 client.query DeprecationWarning
description: Backend npm test and venice:probe emit 'DeprecationWarning: Calling client.query() when the client is already executing a query' once per process under X13 Prisma 7 + @prisma/adapter-pg migration. node-postgres reusing a single Client for overlapping queries inside Prisma $transaction. Resolution paths: (1) wait for @prisma/adapter-pg release using pg.Pool-per-statement; (2) bump pg@^9 if Prisma adapter peer-declares it; (3) if neither lands within ~6 weeks, file upstream issue with minimal repro and add scoped process.removeAllListeners('warning') filter for that exact code. Do NOT add global node-warning silencer in tests.
notes:
verify: cd backend && npm test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/backend-test.log && ! grep -q 'client.query() when the client is already executing' /tmp/backend-test.log
ref: TASKS.md [M1] (was X16) (backlog next)

(3) Per-model Reset button clears user overrides for that model and falls back to model defaults. (4) Some Venice models expose default param values via /models endpoint — load as baseline defaults instead of global hardcode.

title: [X30] @-mention character picker always shows 'No characters'
description: @-mention character picker inside editor always shows 'No characters in this story yet.' even when story has characters. Suspected timing/provider-registration race in frontend/src/lib/charRefSuggestion.ts + frontend/src/hooks/useCharRefSuggestionProvider.ts + frontend/src/components/Paper.tsx. Suggestion extension created with module-level provider callback; Paper registers in mount effect; useCharactersQuery resolves later. Investigation: (1) reproduce with ≥1 character; (2) confirm useCharactersQuery resolves; (3) decide between deferring suggestion init, passing chars through TipTap config, or re-triggering menu when provider/data appears.
notes:
verify: cd frontend && npx vitest run tests/components/CharRefSuggestion.test.tsx
ref: TASKS.md [X30] (backlog next)
root-cause: useActiveStoryStore was orphaned — Paper read activeStoryId from a store whose setter was never called anywhere. Fix threaded storyId as a Paper prop from EditorPage; deleted the dead store + its test.

title: Phase 2: hand-written docs/session-log.md cross-session digest
description:
Phase 2 of docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. Blocked qualitatively on the Phase 1 → Phase 2 gate.

Add docs/session-log.md as an append-only file with 5–10 line digests of completed sessions. The plan explicitly chose hand-written over auto-summarised because auto-summaries decay too fast.

Initial entry: a one-paragraph note from whoever lands this ticket (the seed entry).

Subsequent sessions: read the top 3 entries on startup. Verifiable by inspecting the main-session prompt (the SessionStart staleness hook can grow a pointer once #5/#6 land, but no automation needed for v1 — just the file + a CLAUDE.md / agent-workflow.md cross-reference telling future sessions to read it).
notes:
plan: docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md Phase 2 (gate: qualitative, ~10 real /bd-execute runs)
verify: test -f docs/session-log.md && [ $(wc -l < docs/session-log.md) -ge 5 ]

title: bd: encode surviving CLAUDE.md "Task Order" hard-gates as blocked-by edges
description:
Phase 1 leftover from docs/multi-agent-workflow-plan.md. CLAUDE.md's historical "Task Order" section lists hard gates between bring-up letters (B requires AU, narrative CRUD requires E3+E9, V requires AU11+AU12, L requires V17, F AI features require V5+, E2E requires Docker Compose). The plan retires this section in favour of bd's blocked-by graph, with the rationale already preserved in docs/agent-workflow.md ("Preserved Task-Order rationale" section).

What's left:
1. Walk the surviving hard-gates; identify which are still live (most letters are archived; F Phase 4, X, M, DS sections remain in scope per CLAUDE.md "Task Order").
2. Map live gates to current open bd issues; encode as bd dep add <issue> <prereq>.
3. Some gates may be entirely satisfied (every prerequisite letter archived) and need no encoding.
4. Delete the prescriptive prose from CLAUDE.md, leaving only a one-line pointer to docs/agent-workflow.md.

Lint check for the prose-deletion half: `! grep -E "Hard gates|do not start until" CLAUDE.md`. The bd-graph half is judgment work and won't have a clean automated verify.
notes:
plan: needs design — research live gates first, then encode + prune
verify: TBD — judgment work; prose-deletion half can use `! grep -E "Hard gates|do not start until" CLAUDE.md`

Decide: (a) turn web search OFF for all inline AI (simplest); (b) keep ON as silent fact-grounding nudge (tolerable but undocumented); (c) extend V26 delivery to inline AI (requires F-design for sources UI on inline card). Write decision into docs/venice-integration.md § Web Search. If (a), remove enableWebSearch from ai.routes.ts body schema + update docs/api-contract.md.

title: [X34] Venice call observability
description: AI/chat routes only console.error on failure — zero log line for successful Venice call, so smoke-tests cannot confirm what was sent. Recommendation: dev-only one-line console.log in ai.routes.ts + chat.routes.ts before client.chat.completions.create printing { userId, modelId, action, max_completion_tokens, model_cap, user_setting, enable_web_search }, gated on NODE_ENV !== 'production'. Optionally add x-debug-* response headers opportunistically. Defer pino/structured logger until needed. MUST NOT log decrypted Venice key, decrypted narrative content, or assembled messages array.
notes:
verify: cd backend && npx tsc --noEmit && cd ../frontend && npx tsc -b
ref: TASKS.md [X34] (recently added; pure prod-gated dev logging — typecheck is the meaningful gate until the dev-log assertion test ships)
